question: Is the text on the record cover spelled exactly as "Album: Paint the Night in Shades of Blue — Where Silence Speaks and You Find You" (including the colon and em dash)?
Is the record cover text rendered in a retro serif font style?
Is the text on the wall sign spelled exactly as "RECORDING IN SESSION KEEP IT DOWN CLOSE THE DOOR BEHIND YOU"?
Is the "RECORDING IN SESSION..." text rendered as a glowing neon tube sign?
Is the text on the sheet music spelled exactly as "Tempo = 120 BPM  Time Sig = 4/4  Repeat from Bar 3 to Bar 8" (including equals signs and numbers)?
Is the sheet music text rendered in a printed type style on paper?
Is the text on the mixing console label spelled exactly as "Vocal Track: Denoise first, add subtle reverb, then apply light compression"?
Is the mixing console label text rendered in a handwriting style on paper tape?
Is the text on the cassette tape sticker spelled exactly as "Demo 2026-02-05  Track 1: Take It Slow  Track 2: No Rush" (including the date format and colons)?
Is the cassette tape sticker text rendered in a typewriter font?
Is the text on the T-shirt spelled exactly as "All the world's a stage and we are merely players" (including the apostrophe)?
Is the T-shirt text rendered in a bold font style?
Is the text on the mug spelled exactly as "Formula: Inspiration = Observation + Practice + Time ≠ Pulling All-Nighters" (including the ≠ symbol)?
Is the mug text rendered as transfer-printed text that follows the curvature of the mug?
Is the text on the receipt spelled exactly as "Intimate Show  Doors 8:30 PM  Assigned Seating  No Seat Saving Please"?
Is the receipt text rendered in a thermal print font style?
Is the receipt visibly pinned to the wall with a thumbtack?
Are all text characters well-formed without missing strokes, merged letters, or garbled symbols?
Is all text clearly legible and distinct, avoiding blurring into the claymation/plastic textures?
Are all eight distinct text carriers (record, neon sign, sheet music, tape label, cassette, T-shirt, mug, receipt) present in the scene?
Does the overall scene maintain a claymation style with soft squishy textures and exaggerated lighting?
